Hero.systemOnline: Open to EU-remote roles · product-minded full-stack
Hero.title:
Ship the product.
Earn the room.
Hero.subtitle: I'm Mohammad Afsharfar — I go by Nima. I ship outcomes you can verify: React & Node platforms, data and automation pipelines, and research-backed decision systems (thesis: OptiSupply — explainable ESG scoring aligned with expert benchmarks, r ≈ 0.81). This site is proof for hiring teams and collaborators (live demos, repos, case studies). Commissioned builds: start from Contact with goals and constraints.
Hero.openToRoles: Business Analyst · Product / Program · Tech-driven Strategy

Services.title: Where I go deepest.
Services.subtitle: Two hemispheres, one thread: I translate between engineering and stakeholders. Whether you care about conversion-ready UI, governance-grade dashboards, or how research (DSR / TAM extensions) lands in software — here's how I plug in.

small.name: Product surfaces & marketing web
small.description: High-impact pages and lightweight apps that load fast, rank well, and feel premium.

business.name: Dashboards & internal tools
business.description: Operational views, role-based access, and integrations that keep teams aligned.

features.4: Hardening for real traffic and edge cases

saas.name: Full-stack product slices
saas.description: MVPs and vertical slices that prove the model before you scale the org.

features.4: Deploy pipelines tuned for iteration speed

retainer.name: Ongoing craft & mentorship
retainer.description: For teams that want an experienced eye on architecture, reviews, and incremental delivery.

TechStack.title: Stack I reach for most days

SelectedResults.title: Selected impact
SelectedResults.subtitle: Fast signals a hiring manager can scan in 10 seconds.

items.0: Built an AI-based ESG decision system with ~0.81 correlation to expert evaluation.
items.1: Developed full-stack platforms (React, Node.js, MongoDB) from idea to deployment.
items.2: Led cross-functional initiatives improving internal workflows and coordination.
items.3: Combined MBA-level business strategy with software engineering execution.

TargetRoles.title: Where to place me on the org chart
TargetRoles.intro: If the role needs translation between code and business — and a bias toward shipped demos — we're aligned.

roles.0: Business analyst & digital transformation roles (requirements, process, stakeholder bridges)
roles.1: Product / program roles with delivery ownership (roadmaps, thin slices, cross-functional rhythm)
roles.2: Tech-driven strategy & internal tooling (dashboards, data ops, governance-aware software)

About.title:
Engineer · strategist
· researcher.
About.subtitle: Budapest-based. Iranian. BSc in software engineering (strong GPA), MBA in management (Merit), and founder of NomadSpot. I've worked on digital and technical initiatives for a major retail holding, co-founded e-commerce, contributed at Zoomit-scale traffic, and completed thesis-grade work on explainable AI for sustainable supply chains.

founder.role: Founder NomadSpot · MBA (Management) · strategic tech & delivery background
founder.p1: I bridge engineering and stakeholder rooms: co-founded Shoppermo, worked across cross-functional programs at Arash Holding, shipped high-traffic features at Zoomit, and now build NomadSpot (React, Node.js, MongoDB).
founder.p2: Core stack: React/Next, Node + REST, Python for data/automation, SQL/NoSQL, and production-minded observability. Business side: digital transformation, stakeholder communication, and ESG/procurement analytics.
founder.p3: I prefer thin vertical slices, written trade-offs, and demos that reduce uncertainty early.
founder.p4: Languages: English (fluent, academic & professional), Persian (native), Hungarian (foundational). EU-friendly hours; async-first with crisp writing.

credentials.0: MBA (Management) · Budapest Metropolitan University · merit classification
credentials.1: BSc Computer Engineering (Software) · Islamic Azad University · GPA 17.60/20
credentials.2: Certified specialist · Python & web architecture (MFT) — top scores
credentials.3: Thesis-grade artifact: OptiSupply — explainable ESG decision support (r≈0.81 vs experts)

global.title: From regulation to release notes
global.desc: Comfortable translating EU AI governance, CSRD/ESRS pressures, and supply-chain risk language into backlog items engineers can estimate.

speed.title: Operational velocity
speed.desc: Modern stacks with automated checks — the same discipline that kept retail and media platforms running when traffic and politics both spike.

partners.title: Embedded, not ornamental
partners.desc: I work with product, marketing, and ops as peers — documentation and handover are part of the definition of done.

About.lookingFor: Currently looking for roles in Business Analysis, Product / Program, or Technology Strategy in international (EU-friendly) environments.

cta.title: Prefer browsing first?
cta.desc: Jump into case studies with live previews and narrative breakdowns.

Work.title: Selected work
Work.subtitle: Each project uses the same structure: what I built → what I did → result. Live or interactive demos are included where possible.
Work.empty: No work published yet. Add your first case study from the admin panel.

Start.title: Beyond the PDF
Start.subtitle: Résumés flatten a zig-zag career. Here: how I collaborate when stakes are real — tuned for recruiters who read both the MBA column and the GitHub graph.
Start.headline: I work best with teams that value writing, async clarity, and demos over decks.
Start.intro: If you're hiring: skim Work for proof, About for background, then ping me with the role and stack. If you're a founder: tell me the riskiest assumption you're testing — I'll reply with how I'd tackle it in week one.

card1.title: Proof over promises
card1.desc: Live previews, screenshots, and bilingual write-ups so reviewers see the real surface area.

card2.title: Stack transparency
card2.desc: Next.js, TypeScript, Prisma, Postgres, modern CSS — boring on purpose so teams know what they're getting.

card3.title: Timezone friendly
card3.desc: Budapest-based, EU-friendly hours, comfortable collaborating US ↔ EU with written rituals.

Process.title: How I run an engagement
Process.subtitle: A lightweight loop you can map to sprint calendars — discovery first, thin vertical slices, instrumentation early.

discovery.title: Discover
discovery.desc: Align on outcomes, constraints, and risk — usually async docs plus a focused workshop.

design.title: Shape
design.desc: Flows, UI states, and API contracts sketched tight enough to estimate without hand-wavy magic.

development.title: Build
development.desc: Vertical slices with tests where they matter, telemetry where it informs decisions.

launch.title: Ship & learn
launch.desc: Release, watch metrics, iterate — with runbooks so your team isn't locked to me.

CTA.title: Let's compare notes — technical or strategic.
CTA.subtitle: Share the role, constraints, and what “success” looks like in week 12. I'll reply with relevant work (code + business context) and a sane first milestone.

intro.title: 1. Introduction
intro.content: Welcome to NIMA Studio. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This privacy policy will inform you as to how we look after your personal data when you visit our website and tell you about your privacy rights and how the law protects you.

data.title: 2. Data We Collect
data.content: We may collect, use, store and transfer different kinds of personal data about you which we have grouped together follows:

items.identity: Identity Data includes first name, last name, username or similar identifier.
items.contact: Contact Data includes email address and telephone number.
items.technical: Technical Data includes internet protocol (IP) address, browser type and version, time zone setting and location.

usage.title: 3. How We Use Your Data
usage.content: We will only use your personal data when the law allows us to. Most commonly, we will use your personal data in the following circumstances:

items.contract: Where we need to perform the contract we are about to enter into or have entered into with you.
items.legitimate: Where it is necessary for our legitimate interests (or those of a third party) and your interests and fundamental rights do not override those interests.

agreement.title: 1. Agreement to Terms
agreement.content: By accessing our website at nimastudio.com, you agree to be bound by these terms of service, all applicable laws and regulations, and agree that you are responsible for compliance with any applicable local laws.

license.title: 2. Use License
license.content: Permission is granted to temporarily download one copy of the materials (information or software) on NIMA Studio's website for personal, non-commercial transitory viewing only.

disclaimer.title: 3. Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: The materials on NIMA Studio's website are provided on an 'as is' basis. NIMA Studio makes no warranties, expressed or implied, and hereby disclaims and negates all other warranties including, without limitation, implied warranties or conditions of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement of intellectual property or other violation of rights.

Project.readyDesc: Reach out with the role or product context — I respond personally.
Project.livePreviewHint: Scroll inside the frame to explore the full page, like a real browser window.
Project.showcaseTerminalHint: Illustrative sample output — not a live or production connection.
Project.repoMetaUnavailable: Live stats could not be loaded (private repo or rate limit). You can still open the repository from the button above.
Project.interactiveLabFootnote: Sample JSON from this site’s /api/portfolio-demo route — not your local Python runtime.

Footer.description: Mohammad Afsharfar (Nima) — engineer, MBA graduate, founder. Full-stack delivery, data & automation, ESG-informed analytics. Budapest · open to EU-remote roles. Solo practice (NIMA Studio): not a multi-person agency — depth over headcount.
Footer.bannerSubtitle: For roles: send the title, stack, and timeline. For projects: send goals and constraints. I read everything and reply within a few days.
